channel.title: Programming on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Programming on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: The Credits Problem, Part 2
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the credits problem, part two.

item.title: Code Comments
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about code comments.

item.title: Spaghetti Code
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about spaghetti code, what it is, why it exists, what people say about it?

item.title: Challenges Facing An Arcanum Remaster
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about challenges facing an, Arcanum remaster.

item.title: Fallout's Memory Model
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout’s memory model.

item.title: Fallout Foot Sliding: A Case Study in R&D
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout Footsliding, which I think is a good case study of R&D, especially in game development in the 1980s and 1990s.

item.title: Challenges Making A Fallout Remaster
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the challenges making of making a Fallout remaster.

item.title: Event Systems
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Event Systems.

item.title: Status Effect Implementation
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about status effect implementation.

item.title: Dynamic Quest Design
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Dynamic Quest Design.

item.title: Code Refactoring
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about code refactoring.

item.title: Arcanum Optimization
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Arcanum optimization.

item.title: Code The Rules, Script The Exceptions
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about coding rules for your game and using scripts for the exceptions.

item.title: Arcanum "Dat" Files
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about arcanum’s dat files.

item.title: Error Handling
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about error handling.

item.title: Common Sources Of Programming Frustration
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about common sources of programming frustration.

item.title: Programming Languages
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim.

item.title: Loading And Saving
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about loading and saving games.

item.title: The "Best" OS For Game Development
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about the best operating system to make games.

item.title: Implementing Endgame Slides
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk to you about implementing endgame slides.

item.title: Code Organization
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about how I structure code, and this was a question from a lot of different people, including greenia 84, who asked that I do it using an example of inventory, which I think is a good question or good uh specification, because inventory contains a number of interesting things about code structure and it’s common to a lot of RPGs, so I’ll talk about that now.

item.title: Building Tools
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about building tools.

item.title: Telemetry
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Telemetry in games.

item.title: The Evolution Of Source Control
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about the evolution of source control.

item.title: Arcanum As A Windows 10 App
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Arcanum.

item.title: Combat Coding
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about combat, like technical how to code combat kind of thing, like I’m going to talk data structures and event handling.

item.title: Organizing Game Code
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how I structure code, and I don’t really mean like I’m going to break down code into structures.

item.title: Programmer Roles Explained
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer another question, this one from ATA 94, who asks: can you tell me what is the difference between a lead position and a so-called not lead position?

item.title: Quest Implementation
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about Quest implementation.

item.title: Developing Tyranny
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the development of Tyranny.

item.title: My Work Pattern For Programming
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my work pattern while coding.

item.title: Optimization
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about optimization.

item.title: Implementing Randomness
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about randomness.

item.title: Challenges Facing Game Programmers
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to continue from yesterday’s video about challenges designers face.

item.title: Making of Bard's Tale Construction Set (1991)
item.description: Hi. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the making of Bard’s tail construction set.

item.title: GNW & TIG, My OS Abstraction Libraries
item.description: Hi. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about gnaw and Tig, that’s gnaw gnw and Tig These are operating system abstraction libraries that I wrote in the 90s to basically hide away all the ugliness and inconsistencies and just kind of dumbness of the operating systems on various microcomputers at the time.

item.title: Coding Toys (Insomnia Edition)
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim.

item.title: Troika's Secret Fourth Project
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me Tim.
